source_text:
11. Enclosures. Refer to each enclosure in the text of the letter. If there is more than one enclosure, number them according to the order mentioned in the text. If only one enclosure is listed, do not precede it with a number, i.e., Encl: (1). If you send material separate from the letter, note it, i.e., Encl: (1) COMTHIRDFLT MSG DTG 112200Z JAN 90 (SEP COVER). An enclosure is a document, publication, or other item forwarded with a letter. To avoid confusion, do not list the same document as both a reference and an enclosure.

a. Placement. Type "Encl:" at the left margin on the second line below the last line of the signature block. Do not abbreviate "Encl:."

EXAMPLE:
Encl: (1) JAG letter Ser 13/1234 of 4 May 1992
     (2) NAVSEA ltr Ser 05/0056 of 6 Apr 1992
     (3) NAVCOMPT ltr Ser 03/0001 of 27 Mar 1992

b. List of Enclosures When No Text is Written. If enclosures make up the correspondence package (i.e., the basic correspondence does not include text), place the list of enclosures at the left margin on the second line below the "From:" line.

EXAMPLE:
From: Commanding Officer, Naval Station, Guam
To: Commander, Naval Base, Pearl Harbor

Encl: (1) COMNAVMARIANAS INST 5040.1
     (2) CINCPACFLTINST 5040.2
     (3) NAVSTA Guam ltr Ser 00/0001 of 1 Jan 1992
plain_language_rule: Refer to each enclosure in the text of the letter. List enclosures after the signature block using "Encl:" at the left margin — never abbreviate to "Encl" or "Enc". Number multiple enclosures in the order they appear in the text; a single enclosure is listed without a preceding number. If material is sent separate from the letter, note it as separate cover (SEP COVER). Do not list the same document as both a reference and an enclosure. For correspondence without body text, place the enclosure list at the left margin on the second line below the "From:" line.
renderer_impact: Place "Encl:" at left margin, second line below signature block; indent subsequent enclosure items (5 spaces in examples); for non-text letters, place second line below "From:" line; single enclosure has no number prefix
notes: A single enclosure is listed WITHOUT a preceding number — the parenthetical "i.e., Encl: (1)" in the manual illustrates what NOT to do. The "SEP COVER" notation is placed at the end of the enclosure description. No continuation/fold rules for multi-page enclosures are specified in this section. Each enclosure description is indented relative to "Encl:" (5 spaces in the examples). "Do not abbreviate 'Encl:'" is an explicit formatting rule. The mutual exclusion rule (no document may be both a reference and an enclosure) is absolute.
